summary: Cathy O'Neil shares two pieces of personal news: the release of her book cover for 'Weapons of Math Destruction' and an upcoming live performance with her bluegrass band. She playfully obsesses over her book's Amazon ranking despite promising herself she wouldn't, finding humor in the absurdity of ranking metrics. The post serves as both a book pre-order announcement and an invitation to see her band play at a Brooklyn venue.
key_points: The book cover for 'Weapons of Math Destruction' has been publicly released
O'Neil admits to tracking Amazon rankings despite her self-promise not to, showing self-aware humor about metrics obsession
Her bluegrass band, the Tomtown Ramblers, is playing their first public venue gig
The post reveals O'Neil's life outside data science criticism - she plays music and values community
money_quotes: I'm proud to say that, more than six months before release date, it is the 714th best selling book in the category of statistical books inside of Educational & Reference books inside Business & Money books!
It's also the 860,121th best selling book overall. Considering there are more than 129 million books overall, that's pretty fucking amazing.
Maybe my book will be the 130 millionth book of all time.
Think of it as a church alternative, but with booze.
Our harmonies are amazing and we err on the side of murder ballads.
key_insight: Even someone who critiques algorithmic metrics for a living can't resist obsessing over their own Amazon ranking, revealing the universal human vulnerability to quantified validation.
